title: Close (CanClose) [APS]
text: Close (CanClose) [APS] Window property of an application shell that determines how the application window is closed. CanClose = On | Off | Async | AppShell Value Description Default Undefined. The default will be used. On The user can close the application window by clicking the Close button in the title bar or choosing Close from the system menu, or by pressing Alt+F4 . Off The user can only close the application by pressing Cancel or ^Quit (Gold+Q). The Close button (if visible) is disabled. Async The Async Interrupt trigger of the current component is activated when the application is closed. App Shell The Async Interrupt trigger of the application shell is activated when the application is closed. Usage Dynamic? No Dependencies: Caption and System Menu must be True . Description A Close button is visible only if System Menu is true . If Close is False , the button is disabled. If you choose Asynch or Application Shell , you need to ensure that the appropriate Async Interrupt trigger contains the code to be performed when the application exits. Applies to Application Shells for Desktop Applications Related Links System Menu (SysMenu) Window property of a form or application shell that determines whether the Windows system menu and buttons to minimize, maximize, and close the window are available for this window. Valid values: True or False. Caption Window property that determines whether the form window has a title bar. Valid values: True (default) or False. trigger receiveMessage Reacts to asynchronous messages that come from outside the application. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
